title: Resolve whether decision threads replace the shared log

entries.body: The shared log already carries lightweight narrative context; the new surface should only carry structured async decisions.

evidence.summary: Log entry captures discovery context and the live claim opening.
Open example shows how concrete async questions stay in a decision thread.
Napkin remains the place for session learning and surprises.

entries.body: Should decision threads replace ordinary log notes for routine discovery?
No. Keep discovery notes in the shared log; use decision threads only for structured async overlap discussion and explicit decisions.
Decision threads complement the shared log and active claims; they do not replace either surface.
